name: pica_apollo_search_organizations_company_search
description: [APOLLO] Search Organizations (Company Search) on apollo

query.description: All filters for this POST endpoint must be provided as query parameters (not in the body).

currently_using_any_of_technology_uids[].description: Filter by technologies currently used. Apollo supports 1,500+ technologies. Use underscores (`_`) to replace spaces/periods as listed in Apollo’s supported technologies CSV. Examples: `salesforce`, `google_analytics`, `wordpress_org`.

latest_funding_amount_range[max].description: Max amount for the most recent funding round. Use with `latest_funding_amount_range[min]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `5000000`, `15000000`.

latest_funding_amount_range[min].description: Min amount for the most recent funding round. Use with `latest_funding_amount_range[max]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `5000000`, `15000000`.

latest_funding_date_range[max].description: Latest date of most recent funding round (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `latest_funding_date_range[min]`. Example: `2025-09-25`.

latest_funding_date_range[min].description: Earliest date of most recent funding round (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `latest_funding_date_range[max]`. Example: `2025-07-25`.

organization_ids[].description: Include only these Apollo organization IDs. Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`.

organization_job_locations[].description: Locations of active job postings. Examples: `atlanta`, `japan`.

organization_job_posted_at_range[max].description: Latest job posted date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `organization_job_posted_at_range[min]`. Example: `2025-09-25`.

organization_job_posted_at_range[min].description: Earliest job posted date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `organization_job_posted_at_range[max]`. Example: `2025-07-25`.

organization_locations[].description: HQ location filter (cities, US states, countries). Results are based on HQ location (not office locations). Examples: `texas`, `tokyo`, `spain`.

organization_not_locations[].description: Exclude organizations by HQ location. Examples: `minnesota`, `ireland`, `seoul`.

organization_num_employees_ranges[].description: Employee count ranges. Each range is "min,max" (comma-separated, no spaces). You can pass multiple ranges. Examples: `1,10`, `250,500`, `10000,20000`.

organization_num_jobs_range[max].description: Maximum number of active job postings. Use with `organization_num_jobs_range[min]`. Examples: `50`, `500`.

organization_num_jobs_range[min].description: Minimum number of active job postings. Use with `organization_num_jobs_range[max]`. Examples: `50`, `500`.

q_organization_domains_list[].description: Domain(s) for the organization. Do not include `www.`, `@`, etc. Accepts up to 1,000 domains per request. Examples: `apollo.io`, `microsoft.com`.

q_organization_job_titles[].description: Job titles found in active job postings at the company. Examples: `sales manager`, `research analyst`.

q_organization_keyword_tags[].description: Filter by keyword tags associated with companies. Examples: `mining`, `sales strategy`, `consulting`.

q_organization_name.description: Filter by organization name (partial matches supported). Example: `apollo` or `mining`.

revenue_range[max].description: Maximum revenue. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Example: `50000000`.

revenue_range[min].description: Minimum revenue. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Example: `300000`.

total_funding_range[max].description: Max total funding across all rounds. Use with `total_funding_range[min]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `50000000`, `350000000`.

total_funding_range[min].description: Min total funding across all rounds. Use with `total_funding_range[max]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `50000000`, `350000000`.

query.title: Query Parameters

query.description: Filters must be sent as query parameters in the URL (not in the body).

currently_using_any_of_technology_uids[].description: Filter for employers using any specified technologies. Use underscores instead of spaces/periods.

include_similar_titles.description: Whether to include people with titles similar to person_titles[]. Set to false to return only strict matches when using person_titles[].

organization_job_locations[].description: Locations of jobs being actively recruited by the employer.

organization_job_posted_at_range[max].description: Latest date when jobs were posted. Use with organization_job_posted_at_range[min].

organization_job_posted_at_range[min].description: Earliest date when jobs were posted. Use with organization_job_posted_at_range[max].

organization_locations[].description: HQ location of the person’s current employer (cities, states, countries). If a company has multiple offices, filtering is still based on HQ.

organization_num_jobs_range[max].description: Maximum number of active job postings at the employer. Use with organization_num_jobs_range[min].

organization_num_jobs_range[min].description: Minimum number of active job postings at the employer. Use with organization_num_jobs_range[max].

person_locations[].description: Where people live (cities, US states, countries). To filter by employer HQ location instead, use organization_locations[].

person_seniorities[].description: Seniority levels for a person’s current job title. Matches if any one seniority matches.

person_titles[].description: Job titles held by the people you want to find. People only need to match 1 title. Adding more expands results. Also matches similar terms (e.g., marketing manager may match content marketing manager).

q_keywords.description: A string of words over which to filter results.

q_organization_domains_list[].description: Employer domain(s) (current or previous). Do not include www. or @. Up to 1,000 domains per request.

q_organization_job_titles[].description: Job titles listed in active job postings at the person’s current employer.

revenue_range[max].description: Maximum employer revenue. Use with revenue_range[min]. No currency symbols/commas/decimals.

name: pica_apollo_add_contacts_to_a_sequence
description: [APOLLO] Add Contacts to a Sequence on apollo

sequence_id.description: The Apollo ID for the sequence to which you want to add contacts. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

query.description: Query parameters for this action. `emailer_campaign_id` and `send_email_from_email_account_id` are required. Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

add_if_in_queue.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they are currently in the queue for processing. Default: `false`.

auto_unpause_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when paused contacts should be automatically unpaused. Must be used with `status=paused`.

contact_ids[].description: Apollo IDs for contacts to add. Example item: `66e34b81740c50074e3d1bd4`. Note: Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

contact_verification_skipped.description: Set to `true` to skip contact verification during the addition process. Default: `false`.

contacts_without_ownership_permission.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if you don't have ownership permission for them. Default: `false`.

emailer_campaign_id.description: The same ID as the `sequence_id`. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

label_names[].description: Alternative to `contact_ids[]`. Contacts with these label names will be added. Note: Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

send_email_from_email_account_id.description: Apollo ID for the email account used to send emails to the added contacts. Example: `6633baaece5fbd01c791d7ca`.

send_email_from_email_address.description: Optional specific email address to send from within the email account.

sequence_active_in_other_campaigns.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they have been added to other sequences (does not differentiate active vs paused). Default: `false`.

sequence_finished_in_other_campaigns.description: Set to `true` to add contacts if they are marked as `finished` in another sequence. Default: `false`.

sequence_job_change.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they recently changed jobs. Default: `false`.

sequence_no_email.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they do not have an email address. Default: `false`.

sequence_same_company_in_same_campaign.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if other contacts from the same company are already in the sequence. Default: `false`.

sequence_unverified_email.description: Set to `true` to add contacts if they have an unverified email address. Default: `false`.

status.description: Initial status for added contacts. Enum: `active`, `paused`. If `paused` with `auto_unpause_at`, enables scheduled addition.

user_id.description: Apollo user ID of the user taking the action (for activity log). Example: `66302798d03b9601c7934ebf`.
